entry.title: SAP Installation Server and Microsoft Global Secure Access
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have created a SAP Installation Server for Windows SAP GUI using a virtual machine on Azure, following the guides SAPGUI Installation Server Part 1 - Getting Starte... - SAP Community , SAP GUI Installation Server - SAP Community , SAP GUI for Windows | SAP Help Portal . "Accounts: Guest account status" is enabled on the server as well as "Network Access: Let Everyone permissions apply to anonymous users", using group policy.

I have created a test machine using a virtual machine on Azure on the same vnet on the same subnet. Both virtual machines are joined on the same active directory domain.  All network connections are open between the virtual machines. I have created a package on the server and then installed the package on the client. I changed the package version on the server, and restarted the SAP Update service on the client and automatic workstation update service worked as expected. The new version of the package was dispatched on the client, as expected. 

Then, I performed another test using a windows 11 client that is joined only on Entra (former Azure Active Directory), is managed by InTune, but the users on the PC are hybrid identities (i.e. identities reside both on Active Directory and are also synced on Entra). The client is using Global Secure Access client (What is Global Secure Access? - Global Secure Access | Microsoft Learn) to access the SMB share of the installation server via an application proxy. (Application proxy documentation - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn). The SMB share is accessible after MFA authentication using Entra's Conditional Access. 

I managed to get read access on the share and run NwSapSetup.exe and then installed the predefined package. (The user on the PC had administrative privileges).

Then I changed the package version on the server and restarted  SAP Automatic Workstation Update service in order to check if the package is updated automatically. Unfortunatelly, it did not work. The system promted for logging in to Global Secure Access in order to grant access to the share, but after successful authentication the package was not updated. After checking the NwSapAutoWorkstationUpdateService.log on the client:

Logfile: C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SapSetup\LOGs\NwSapAutoWorkstationUpdateService.log
Started logging: Sat Aug 17 11:07:05 2024 (Θερινή ώρα GTB)
Operating system: Windows 11 Professional (Build 22621 - Version 22H2)
Executing user: SYSTEM (Administrator)
Workstation name: SAPInstallationServerClientMachine
Windows directory: C:\WINDOWS
Temp directory: C:\WINDOWS\TEMP
Working directory: C:\WINDOWS\system32

Commandline: '"C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SAPsetup\Setup\Updater\NwSapAutoWorkstationUpdateService.exe"'
Executable: 'C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SAPsetup\Setup\Updater\NwSapAutoWorkstationUpdateService.exe'
Version: 9.0.119.0
ProcessId: 19588
ThreadId: 16244
Physical memory: 5020 MB of 16069 MB free
System Uptime: 1 day, 19:51:26 hours

System libraries information
----------------------------

atl.dll: 3.5.2284.0
comctl32.dll: 5.82.22621.3527
vbscript.dll: 5.812.10240.16384
msvcrt.dll: 7.0.22621.2506
oleaut32.dll: 10.0.22621.3672
shell32.dll: 10.0.22621.3880
shlwapi.dll: 10.0.22621.3672
msi.dll: 5.0.22621.3880

SAPSetup libraries information
-------------------------------

NwSapSetupEngine.dll: 9.0.119.0
NwSapSetupATLCommon.dll: 9.0.119.0
NwSapSetupUi.dll: 9.0.119.0
NwSapFeiUt.dll: 9.0.119.0
NwSapFeiLg.dll: 9.0.119.0

********************************************************************************


11:07:05.301 NwSapsAtlC 1 SapSetup ATL Common Library Loaded
11:07:05.326 NwSapsEngn 1 SapSetup Workstation Engine Library Loaded
11:07:05.339 NwSapFeiUt 1 Process NwSapSetup.exe is not running.
11:07:05.398 NwSapsAtlC 1 Reboot Manager: Evaluated the position of SAPSetup's OnReboot Installation Service as:
Folder 'C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SapSetup\OnRebootSvc'
Service File Path: 'C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SapSetup\OnRebootSvc\NWSAPSetupOnRebootInstSvc.exe'
11:07:05.398 NwSapsAtlC 1 Reading OnReboot Install Service Configuration from 'C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SapSetup\OnRebootSvc'
11:07:05.398 NwSapsAtlC 1 Loaded 0 reboot action records, 0 command(s) to execute on-start, 0 file(s) to delete, 0 file(s) to copy, 0 assembly name(s) and 0 command(s) to execute at end
11:07:05.399 NwSapsAtlC 1 DE Deleted
11:07:05.399 NwSapsAtlC 1 StringTable released
11:07:05.410 NwSapFeiUt 1 Process NwSapSetup.exe is not running.
11:07:05.472 NwAWUServc 1 Will check for updates now...
11:07:05.472 NwSapFeiUt 1 Loading %ProgramFiles%\SAP\SapSetup\Setup\Updater\WkstaUpdater.cfg...
11:07:05.479 NwSapFeiUt 1 Nbr of Servers in section AdminConfiguredServers: 0
11:07:05.480 NwSapFeiUt 1 Nbr of Servers in section SAPSetupIndexedServers: 1
11:07:05.480 NwSapFeiUt 1 Read Server1=\\myserver.mydomain.com\sap\Setup from section SAPSetupIndexedServers
11:07:05.480 NwSapFeiUt 1 Loaded 1 server paths from the source
11:07:05.494 NwSapFeiUt 1 Process NwSapSetup.exe is not running.
11:07:58.182 NwSapsAtlC 1W Server Path \\myserver.mydomain.com\sap\Setup could not be found: Error message: Access is denied. Error code 0x00000005 (5).
11:07:58.182 NwAWUServc 1W Error: \\myserver.mydomain.com\sap\Setup is not a valid SapSetup folder.
11:07:58.182 NwAWUServc 1 No more updates are available.
11:07:58.182 NwAWUServc 1 Service will retry in 24 hours

It seems that service of automatic workstation update service (AWUS), is using a user that cannot be authenticated on the installation server. Switching to the server, I then checked SMB Security event log (Application & Services - Microsoft - Windows - SMBServer - Security), and I am getting 551 (SMB Session Authentication Failure) and 1009 (The server denied anonymous access to the client) event IDs. It seems that the application proxies are trying to access the SMB share using anonymous logon.
User Name: NT AUTHORITY\ANONYMOUS LOGON
Session ID: 0x2C000000007D

I then added NT AUTHORITY\ANONUMOUS LOGON on the installation server's share with full permissions in order to test and the problem still remains.

Any ideas how to make Global Secure Access work with SAP Installation Server for Entra Joined Machines ?

entry.title: Re: Consume HANA views in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
You will have to create some DSP object which maps the HANA views, else DSP will not know there are any, rt? It is not a rework - you do not need to rebuild the VDM in DSP, you just replicate the remote tables to DSP as Views f.e. 

For mass processing I would go for replication flow.
